beats.narration_text: Jambo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of hydrogen stark effect. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of hydrogen stark effect

beats.narration_text: Hydrogen Stark Effect is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Linear splitting of the n = 2 shell under a uniform electric field. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Stark Effect. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: field-slider. Verify: Linear splitting of the n = 2 shell under a uniform electric field. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Stark Effect. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: field-slider. Verify: Linear splitting of the n =

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 09-hydrogen-stark-effect.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: hydrogen stark effect. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0 to 20000000. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 20000000

beats.narration_text: Linear splitting of the n = 2 shell under a uniform electric field. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of hydrogen stark effect. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Hydrogen Stark Effect simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Hydrogen Stark Effect simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

beats.narration_text: Hydrogen Stark Effect — n=2 Shell. Medhavy.
